topic.what: Security properties of C2PA digital provenance system for verifying digital media authenticity
topic.how: Comprehensive security analysis using formal-methods analysis (CPSA), validation experiments with public C2PA assets, conformance program assessment, and protocol specification review
topic.why_matters: C2PA is becoming the dominant tool for digital media provenance against AI-generated fake content with substantial institutional momentum (Google, Meta, Adobe, Microsoft) yet its security guarantees were poorly understood and the paper demonstrates it fails to achieve its stated goals

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of claim signatures bound to their trusted timestamps
characteristics.evidence: Formal-methods analysis proves timestamp not bound to claim signature; adversaries can replace timestamps without detection
characteristics.context: All tested validators fail to detect timestamp replacement attacks

characteristics.what_it_measures: Agreement among conforming validators on validation outcomes
characteristics.evidence: Table 1 shows contradictory results across validators for same assets; Example 2 produces 4 different results from 4 validators
characteristics.context: Only Verifieddit correctly identifies expired certificates; others report Valid or Not Found

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of validators that correctly reject revoked signing certificates
characteristics.evidence: Example 3: 3 of 4 validators (CAI, Adobe, ProofMode) accept Nikon revoked certificate 6 months after revocation
characteristics.context: Nikon revoked certificate September 2025; validators still accept it March 2026

characteristics.what_it_measures: Portion of digital asset protected by cryptographic signature
characteristics.evidence: Exclusion range permits modifications to metadata and manifest without invalidating signature
characteristics.context: C2PA allows exclusion of metadata and manifest portions from hash computation

characteristics.what_it_measures: Technical rigor of conformance program product certification
characteristics.evidence: C2PA conformance program evaluates only product descriptions; no software/hardware review, no security requirements defined
characteristics.context: Pixel 10 Pro certified at Assurance Level 2 despite security limitations

novelty: first comprehensive security analysis of C2PA specifications and ecosystem
first formal-methods (CPSA) analysis of C2PA cryptographic protocols
first empirical evaluation of C2PA validators using real signed assets
demonstrates timestamp substitution attack enabling provenance doubt
identifies conformance program certifies products without source code review
open_problems: enhance validator usability for non-expert users
design more effective validators with consistent behavior
analyze version 2.3 soft-bindings and watermarking security
explore cryptographic strategies for combating misinformation
investigate privacy-preserving revocation checking (CRL vs OCSP tradeoff)
address aging digital assets with expired certificates and obsolete formats
integrate C2PA with reputation and fact-checking services
